name: payment
description: Payment orchestration + saved payment tokens + dispute lifecycle. Answers 'has this invoice been captured, and what payment method did the customer use?'. FUNDAMENTALLY DISTINCT from the invoice module (invoice = money OWED) and the transaction module (transaction = double-entry ledger movement). Payment sits BETWEEN them — it's the record that a specific customer moved money for a specific invoice, using a specific payment method, at a specific instant. Ships FOUR tables: `payments` (successful captures — one row per successful money-in event; references invoice + transaction; carries provider + provider_reference + captured amount + fees; IMMUTABLE after create — financial records are never mutated, only credit-noted), `payment_methods` (saved payment tokens — NEVER card PAN/CVV; provider-scoped token IDs like Stripe pm_... / Paddle payment_method_id; PCI-DSS Level 1 concern; encrypted at rest; deduplicated per customer via fingerprint hash), `payment_intents` (in-flight payment intent objects — mirrors provider intent lifecycle from initialization → confirmation → success/failure/cancelled; 3DS2 step-up flows live here; client_secret encrypted at rest), `payment_disputes` (per-payment dispute — tenant-visible pre-chargeback lifecycle with evidence submission window; may escalate to Chargeback via chargeback module). MultipleInstanceManager pattern via `PaymentProviderManager` with 6 drivers day-1: **Stripe** (Stripe Payments + Stripe Billing + Stripe Radar + 3DS2), **Paddle** (Paddle Classic + Paddle Billing), **Adyen** (Adyen Checkout + Adyen Marketplace), **Square** (Square Checkout + Cash App Pay), **PayPal** (PayPal Checkout + PayPal Braintree + Venmo), **CustomWebhook** (generic HMAC-signed for tenant-run payment gateways like Braintree direct / dLocal / PayFast). Per-provider webhooks captured via `payment.webhook_verify` middleware + dispatched to `<Provider>WebhookHandler` for state reconciliation. 3D Secure 2 / SCA compliance built-in — payment intents lifecycle handles authentication step-up flows via next_action_type + next_action_url. Every successful payment triggers Transaction.record (double-entry ledger: debit cash, credit accounts_receivable) + Invoice.mark_paid (state update) + growth::marketing::CaptureMembershipPurchasedMarketingEvent (attribution). NO card PAN/CVV/magnetic-stripe/PIN on any row (PCI-DSS Level 1). PSD2 + SCA enforced by 3DS2 gate on card transactions above the SCA threshold. Never conflate with invoice (invoice = the bill) or transaction (transaction = the ledger movement) or refund (refund = the reverse-money-out record, separate module).
planned_consumers: refund (Wave 4 sibling — reads captured payments to compute refundable amount + dispatches provider-side refunds)
chargeback (Wave 4 sibling — receives escalated PaymentDispute → Chargeback via chargeback module)
growth::marketing (Wave 5 — every successful payment fires CaptureMembershipPurchasedMarketingEvent for attribution)

packages: guzzlehttp/guzzle ^7.8 (HTTP client for provider dispatch — TLS-1.2+, connection-pooled, request-signable)
owen-it/laravel-auditing ^13.x (compliance-grade audit trail — every write to payments/payment_methods/payment_intents/payment_disputes emits an audit row with 7-year retention; 10y Enterprise)
wraps.rationale: Every provider driver dispatches through a shared Guzzle client with per-provider timeout + TLS enforcement + connection pooling. The `PaymentIntentOrchestrator` composes provider-native intent creation + confirmation + capture through the drivers. Retry semantics are OWNED BY THIS MODULE — Guzzle-level retry is disabled to preserve idempotency + circuit-breaker semantics. laravel-auditing is the codebase-standard audit trait — every finance table uses it (see invoice + transaction + tax modules).

tenant.description: Tenant-facing payment surface. Two audiences: customer routes (POST /payments/intents to create, POST /confirm to confirm via provider SDK, GET /payments/mine, GET /payment-methods/mine, POST /payment-methods to save a token via SetupIntent, DELETE /payment-methods/{method}) and admin+finance routes (GET /payments to list, POST /{payment}/capture for authorization holds, GET/POST/PATCH /payment-providers for provider CRUD, POST /payment-disputes/{dispute}/submit-evidence). Customer confirmation is via the provider SDK client-side — this API never accepts raw card fields (PCI-DSS scope reduction). Every route carries `sanctum` guard + `X-Application-Id` + `entitlement.enforce:payment_capture`.

webhook.description: Public webhook plane. Per-provider inbound endpoints for provider-side state changes (payment succeeded, dispute opened, chargeback filed). Every request is HMAC-verified via `payment.webhook_verify` middleware (Stripe-Signature / Paddle-Signature / Adyen-HmacSignature / etc.); unverifiable requests return 400 without body. No auth guard — the HMAC IS the auth.

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant support tooling. Ops + support review payment status across tenants for escalation cases (failed captures, disputed transactions), audit financial records, and access dispute evidence for compliance investigations. Redacted view — customer PII is hashed unless the caller holds platform.payment.pii.view.
